architecture.summary: A decoder-only transformer with latent depth recurrence: input tokens are embedded by a prelude block into latent space, then a shared recurrent core block iterates on the hidden state (injecting the embedded input at each step), unrolling to arbitrary depth at test-time. A coda block un-embeds the final latent state to produce next-token logits. The recurrent block is trained with randomly sampled iteration counts from a log-normal Poisson distribution; inference backpropagates through only the last iterations (truncated BPTT in depth). Architecture uses sandwich normalization (norm-layer-norm), RoPE attention, gated SiLU MLPs, and an adapter that concatenates initial embedding with current state.

biblio.title: Scaling up Test-Time Compute with Latent Reasoning: A Recurrent Depth Approach

claims.statement: The model can improve its performance on reasoning benchmarks up to a computation load equivalent to 50 billion parameters
GSM8K CoT performance with flexible match reaches 38.13% at depth 32
On HumanEval pass@1, the model achieves 23.17% with recurrence depth 32
Recurrent model at 180B tokens achieves 9.02/10.24 (flexible/strict) on GSM8K CoT, versus fixed-depth baseline at 1.82/2.20
Does not require specialized training data; can work with small context windows; can capture types of reasoning not easily represented in words
Trained for 800 billion tokens at 3.5 billion parameters on Frontier supercomputer with 4096 GPUs
On standard benchmarks, the model lags behind later OLMo models trained on larger, more carefully curated datasets; lags behind dedicated code models like StarCoder2
Recurrent depth models naturally support per-token adaptive compute, self-speculative decoding, and KV-cache sharing at inference time without specialized training or tuning
